description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, roughly triangular stone fragment with a predominantly pale beige to light pink coloration, interspersed with darker reddish and brownish mineral inclusions. The surface is uneven and granular, with visible stratification and small pitted areas, suggesting a sedimentary origin. One face bears handwritten black marker text, likely for identification or cataloging purposes. The edges are jagged and broken, with some smoother worn areas, indicating partial weathering. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a natural obstacle or boundary marker in an urban or landscaped setting.
description: A pale, irregular stone fragment with reddish mineral spots and rough, stratified surfaces, featuring handwritten text on one side.
description_view_0: Flat, broad face with visible reddish inclusions and a small label at the bottom left.
description_view_1: Narrower face with handwritten text 'Unicoi' clearly visible, showing more of the stone's thickness.
description_view_2: Jagged, rough edge with less visible mineral patterning and a more weathered, uneven texture.
description_view_3: Side view highlighting the stone's thickness and layered structure, with subtle color variations.
attribute_location: urban park border or landscaped sidewalk edge
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loosely embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle stratification with scattered reddish mineral spots and faint layering, irregular and natural in appearance.
attribute_weathering: Partially weathered with rough, granular surfaces and some smoother, worn edges; no significant lichen or moss.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements or contrasts with paved surfaces, may be partially surrounded by soil or mulch.
